## Report

**Files changed (all under `apps/rt/`)**

- `src/commands/scan_guards/list.rs` ‚Äî lifted the walk into `collect_pending(root) -> PendingScaffolds { entries, errors }`, now the single owner of traversal + sorting. `run()` is reduced to the JSON-worklist projection; `Pending` and its fields became `pub(crate)`. The dead `#[cfg(test)] run_collect` shim is gone (tests call the real collector). No second walk, no second ignore list.
- `src/commands/doctor/guards_scaffold_check.rs` (new) ‚Äî `doctor --check guards-scaffold`. Follows rt-check-pattern: module doc naming the invocation + fail-open story, `pub fn run(root) -> Option<GuardsScaffoldReport>`, pure `project_uncurated()` split from `build_report()`, sorted vectors, repo-relative paths, no floats/timestamps, in-file tests.
- `src/commands/doctor/mod.rs` ‚Äî module registration.
- `src/commands/doctor/doctor.rs` ‚Äî added `guards-scaffold` to the typed-check selector, `run_typed_check`, the unknown-check hint, the full run (advisory), `render_combined_json` (`guards_scaffold` key, omitted when there is no census), and `guards_scaffold_to_check_result` (WARN only, never FAIL). **No new `run` subcommand** ‚Äî `tests/run_command_surface.rs` passes untouched.
- `src/commands/agent/render/sections.rs` ‚Äî `read_guards_block` prefixes `> NOTE: ‚Ä¶the uncurated /scan scaffold‚Ä¶There are NO project rules below‚Ä¶` when the body still carries `GUARDS_PENDING_OPEN` (single-sourced from `scan_claude`). Prefix, not swap: the raw block survives. Fail-open intact ‚Äî absent file/section still yields `""` (no injection, no bare notice).
- `src/commands/agent/render/mod.rs` ‚Äî `sections` is now `pub mod` (commented as test-face only); only `read_guards_block` was widened to `pub`.
- `tests/doctor_reports_uncurated_rule_scaffolds.rs`, `tests/dispatch_warns_on_uncurated_rules.rs` (new) ‚Äî top-level `#[test]` fns at binary root, both two-sided.

**Non-obvious decisions**

1. The collector also returns an `errors` vector. Without it the check's `scanned_errors` would be structurally always-empty (decorative), and an unreadable `CLAUDE.md` would read as "enriched". The worklist projection ignores it, so `scan-guards-list` output is byte-identical (verified: still `[]` on this repo).
2. "No census" = `.claude/grain.model.json` absent ‚Üí `run` returns `None`, mirroring `capability_drift_check`. Without Wave 1 nothing can carry the sentinel, so "0 uncurated" would be a vacuous green.
3. No `HarnessEvent` is emitted ‚Äî `mustard-core` publishes no event kind for this finding, and adding one is outside apps/rt.
4. Fixturing an unreadable `CLAUDE.md` uses invalid UTF-8, not a directory: `walk()` recurses into a dir named `CLAUDE.md` rather than classifying it.

**Verification**: `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean on all touched files; `cargo test --lib` 1612 passed; both new integration tests pass under `-- --exact`; `run_command_surface` 7/7; live `doctor --check guards-scaffold --format json` ‚Üí `{"ok":true,‚Ä¶}`, exit 0.

**Blocker encountered (resolved, not mine)**: mid-run the lib stopped compiling with 3 `E0061`s in `src/commands/review/analyze_validation.rs` (plus a call site in `pipeline/plan_materialize.rs`) ‚Äî a concurrent edit to a file I do not own. It cleared on retry; no action taken on those files. No doc surface outside `apps/rt` lists doctor check names (only the shipped `plugin/bin/*.exe` artifacts), so nothing outside my scope needs updating.</result>

## Files changed

- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\src\commands\review\analyze_validation.rs`
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\src\commands\pipeline\plan_materialize.rs`
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\src\commands\spec\spec_draft.rs`
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\tests\spec_draft_context_prose.rs` (call-site update)
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\tests\validation_file_refs.rs` (new ‚Äî the three AC tests)

## What was done

1. **Root as a parameter.** `validate(root: &Path, abs_path: &Path, content: &str)` ‚Äî unconditional, no `Option`, no internal fallback. Both `current_dir()` sites are gone; the second one (V4, extended-light) now reuses the *same* `model` path V2 computes, so there is one project root and one model per run. `ref_resolves` takes `root` and uses `root.join(r)` instead of `Path::new(r)`. Callers pass `crate::shared::context::project_dir()`; `plan_materialize` threads down the `project` root `materialize` already holds (`validate_root_spec(project, spec_dir)`).

2+3. **Charset widened and reference test tightened together**, split into two named functions so the rule is legible: `is_path_token_char` (adds `( ) [ ] { } *`) and `is_file_reference`. The rule, stated in full in the module docs: a token is a reference to one concrete file when it is all path chars, carries no pattern metacharacter (`*`, `{`, `}`), no `//` and no `...` elision segment, and its last segment splits into a non-empty stem plus a clean extension that is either known or accompanied by a `/`. Brackets are deliberately *not* pattern chars ‚Äî `app/[slug]` is a literal directory.

I checked the rejected shapes against real specs in this repo rather than inventing them: `.../spec.md` (`btw-plan-rework-fixes/wave-1-rt/spec.md:22`) is flagged *today* from the correct root, and `wave-N-{role}/spec.md` (same file, line 17) plus `plugin/**/*.md` (`wave-2-docs/spec.md:20`) would have become false warnings the moment the charset widened. Bare `.tsx`-style extension mentions were the fourth class.

4. **Three top-level `#[test]` fns** in one new integration binary, each two-sided. The cwd test is the one worth flagging: besides "existing file resolves / absent file warns", it also asserts that `Cargo.toml` ‚Äî which exists in the *process* working directory but not under the passed root ‚Äî is still reported missing. That is a direct proof `current_dir()` is no longer consulted, and it needs no `set_current_dir` (which would race with parallel tests in the same binary).

## Verification

Each AC command run verbatim; all three emit the declared `Expect: 1 passed`:
`test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; ...; 2 filtered out` for all of `validation_resolves_from_any_working_directory`, `validation_sees_paths_with_punctuated_segments`, `validation_does_not_flag_prose_as_a_file`.

## Notes

- **MOLD CONTRACT:** no `{role}-pattern` skill applies. `analyze_validation.rs` is a `commands/review/` run subcommand, not a `doctor/{name}_check.rs`; `rt-check-pattern` is the nearest mold and its `pub fn run(root: &Path)` convention is exactly the direction task 1 moves in. The new file is an integration test, which no mold covers.
- No blockers. The doctor check and dispatch warning (parent spec root causes 4) belong to wave 2 and were left untouched.

<MEMORY>To prove a function stopped reading a process global like current_dir(), assert that an input resolvable ONLY via that global still fails ‚Äî e.g. reference `Cargo.toml` (present in the cargo test cwd, absent under the passed root) and require the warning. Avoids `set_current_dir`, which races with parallel tests in the same binary.</MEMORY></result>
